steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the circle facts first: radii are equal, a diameter sets up a right angle, a tangent is perpendicular to the radius, and central/inscribed angles connect to arcs.

steps.title: Extract the radius from the diameter
steps.content: The diameter is 31 cm, so the radius is $r = \frac{31}{2} = 15.5$ cm.

steps.title: Calculate the volume using the sphere formula
steps.content:
First compute $r^3$:
$$(15.5)^3 = 3,723.875\text{ cm}^3$$
Substitute into the sphere volume formula with $\pi = 3.14159$:
$$V = \frac{4}{3} \times 3.14159 \times 3,723.875 \approx 15,598.52\text{ cm}^3$$

steps.title: Calculate mass and round to the nearest whole number
steps.content:
Mass equals density times volume:
$$\text{mass} = 2.4 \times 15,598.52 \approx 37,436.44\text{ grams}$$
Rounded to the nearest whole number: 37,436 grams.
